chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA. Constitutional Petition No. D- 537 of 2024 PRESENT: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry, Mr. Justice Abdul Hamid Bhurgri, Petitioner: Kareem Bux Brohi, through Mr. Safdar Ali Ghouri Advocate. Respondents: Federation of Pakistan & others, through Mr. Oshaque Ali Sangi, Asst. Attorney General. Mr. Munawar Ali Abbasi, Asst. Advocate General, along with SIP Sibghatullah Jatoi, SHO PS Amrote on behalf of Inspector General of Sindh Police and DIG Police, Larkana Range. Date of Hearing: 26.02.2026. Date of Decision: 26.02.2026. O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry, J.- The petition was filed for the whereabouts of two brothers, namely, Abdul Nabi and Zahoor Ahmed (brothers of the petitioner), who had proceeded to Saudi Arabia in the year 2022 through an overseas employment agency; whereafter, they did not contact their family at Shikarpur, thus prompting this petition that the two brothers are missing persons. From the comments of the Director, Protectorate of Emigrants and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs it transpires that the two brothers were on contract with their Saudi Sponsor (Kafeel) for two years, but they abandoned said sponsor in 6 months; therefore, the sponsor reported the two brothers to 2 the Saudi authorities, and for this reason the two brothers have made themselves scarce. These facts are verified by the employment agency who is in contact with said sponsor and by the Embassy of Pakistan at Riyadh who is in touch with the Saudi authority that is pursuing the two brothers. In view of foregoing facts, the two brothers are not missing persons in the sense alleged by the petitioner.

These facts are verified by the employment agency who is in contact with said sponsor and by the Embassy of Pakistan at Riyadh who is in touch with the Saudi authority that is pursuing the two brothers. In view of foregoing facts, the two brothers are not missing persons in the sense alleged by the petitioner. They are fugitives from Saudi law. Furthermore, this Court does not have any jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution of Pakistan to issue any writ to Saudi authorities in Saudi Arabia. The Embassy of Pakistan at Riyadh also has no jurisdiction to interfere with any investigation or legal action taken or being taken by Saudi authorities against the two brothers. Resultantly, the petition is dismissed as not maintainable. JUDGE JUDGE Qazi Tahir PA/*
